item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit of a weak article, but that last log cements my upvote. Still wish there was more to it, though.

item.title: Re: What should the cookies say?
item.content:encoded: Most of my issues arise from the tone of the last addendum. It just feels off. The punchline is good, though.

item.title: Re: What should the cookies say?
item.content:encoded:
I don't feel like there's much more to say. The Zen thing gets repetitive quickly. What would people put in there?

SCP: Where did you come from?
Cookie: We all come from nothing, and to nothing we return.

SCP: How can we destroy SCP-682?
Cookie: You already know SCP-682 to be destroyed, for all it knows is destruction.

item.title: Re: What should the cookies say?
item.content:encoded:
I suppose I could open up a collaborative extended testing log?

I think that might be a mistake. As it is the cookies are very Zen, and I am not sure that crowdsourced cookies would retain such a clear voice; I rather suspect they wouldn't both because of the nature of crowdsourcing and the cognitive challenge of Zen.

item.title: What should the cookies say?
item.content:encoded:
So it sounds like people want to hear more about what the cookies have to say. However, I'm not entirely sure I know what they should talk about, so I'm not sure what should be changed.

I suppose I could open up a collaborative extended testing log?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fun fact: I eat the fortunes and not the cookies.

(100% true.)

item.title: Re: A home for 2471?
item.content:encoded: The horrifying curse that has claimed the lives of so many…

item.title: Re: A home for 2471?
item.content:encoded:
The boy has a place in the world now, he's… he's an article.

The curse has been broken.

item.title: No vote
item.content:encoded:
Gonna have to agree with Bluemorpho on this one.

As always, this is very well writen and has lotsa creativity behind it. However, it doesn't really does it for me, can't put it into words.

Tho the interview log was cool.

Edit: I started to think it through, and I think that what bugged me was the short interview log. I think this may have been more interesting for me if we could see other things the cookies had to say.

Tho is already at +25 so it doesn't really matters. Good job, WJS!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the last addendum, it almost rounds this out to a fully formed story, or at last thought.

Unfortunately, not quite. There just isn't enough going on here. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this could be improved if we had some more time with the cookies in the (or multiple) interview(s) - it's definitely the strongest part of the article.

I think the recovery log is unnecessary. I feel like we don't always need to know precisely how something fell into the Foundations hands, and for the amount of text that goes into explaining here, all we really get out of it is a crosslink that could be worked in elsewhere for a fraction of the word count.

I'm a thousand times more interested in what the cookies have to say.

item.title: A home for 2471?
item.content:encoded: Finally, after several failed attempts, it's found a home.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I thought this should be a -j, but the interview got me. Some of the fortunes don't seem like something it would actually be said, but for the most part this is pretty good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was coming to no-vote this, but then reading this comment gave me perspective to elevate it to an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't… get this one, I guess? It feels like it's lacking anything in it, and the crosslink felt really shoved in there. I get what the idea is, that fortune cookies are sentient, but I still can't really grasp what the value is in there. It's not funny, it's not sad, it's just like a generic monster skip turned into one about cookies. I do like the idea, and the "About time I got out of that cookie" thing made chuckle a bit, so I'm going to no-vote this, but it just has nothing I could find substance in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Wú".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
because it's my kind of jam

Fun fact: I eat fortune cookies with grape jam (if it's available).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this because it's my kind of jam, and also because I tend to smash fortune cookies into chunks1 rather than cracking them in half. ;(

Footnotes
1. because with big cookie pieces when I bite them into more manageable pieces the pieces go flying everywhere so I try to save myself the trouble…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These cookies have the Buddha nature.
Eh, I rethought my vote. While I appreciate the Series I vibe this is going for, this felt too bare-bones. It was kind of clever, but the last addendum was kind of awkward and tone-breaking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sugarcomb's at it again!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is gonna have to be the rare no-vote on something you post, John. I am really not feeling this one, but it's well-written as always, and admittedly cohesive. I just can't get past the bland nature of the humor — "about time I got out of that cookie" and similar aspects — and the short length.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, when they're getting ready to eat at a restaurant and the book goes off on a tangent about genetically engineered livestock who want nothing more than to be eaten.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
A friend of mine posted the photo, and I was inspired.

Photo is by Velocity Girl, and used with permission.

Thanks to Cyantreuse, Zyn, DrBleep, and AidenEldritch for looking at the draft.
